title: General Project Manifest Schema
description: Manifest for non-software projects (academic, creative, business, personal). Describes project identity, structure, deliverables, and context.
$comment: This schema is intentionally open (no additionalProperties:false). Add sections that best manifest your project's content. Examples: 'research_methodology' for academic, 'brand_guidelines' for creative, 'market_analysis' for business.

project.description: Basic identity, purpose, and timeline of the project.
project.$comment: Core project information. Adapt fields to your domain: academic (thesis title, institution), creative (client, campaign), business (company, market).

name.$comment: Examples: 'Master Thesis: AI in Healthcare', 'Brand Redesign for Acme Corp', 'Q4 Marketing Campaign'.

description.description: High-level description of what this project aims to achieve.
description.$comment: Describe the purpose, audience, and expected outcome. Avoid jargon, write for someone unfamiliar with the project.

start_date.$comment: When work on this project began. Use best estimate if exact date unknown.

target_date.$comment: Optional. Deadline or target completion date if applicable.

stakeholders.description: People or entities involved or interested in the project.
stakeholders.$comment: Examples: 'Prof. García (thesis advisor)', 'Marketing Team', 'Client: Acme Corp', 'Investors'.

structure.description: Organization of the project: sections, chapters, phases, or components.
structure.$comment: Adapt to your project type. Academic: chapters/sections. Creative: deliverables/assets. Business: phases/milestones.

components.description: List of main components, sections, or phases.

description.description: Brief description of this component's purpose.
description.$comment: What this component covers or delivers.

dependencies.description: Other components this one depends on.
dependencies.$comment: Optional. List names of components that must be completed first.

deliverables.description: Concrete outputs or artifacts the project will produce.

name.$comment: Examples: 'Final Thesis Document', 'Brand Guidelines PDF', 'Pitch Deck', 'Market Analysis Report'.

description.description: What this deliverable contains or represents.
description.$comment: Brief description of content and purpose.

due_date.$comment: Optional. Specific deadline for this deliverable.

deliverables.$comment: List all expected outputs. Academic: thesis, presentations. Creative: designs, assets. Business: reports, decks.

references.description: Reference materials, sources, and resources for the project.
references.$comment: Organize references by type. Adapt categories to your domain.

urls.$comment: Examples: 'https://competitor.com', 'Shared Google Drive folder', 'Figma project link'.

bibliography.$comment: For academic projects. Use consistent citation format (APA, MLA, etc.).

standards.description: Guidelines, standards, or methodologies the project follows.
standards.$comment: Reference your project_standards.json or list key standards inline.

style_guide.$comment: Examples: 'APA 7th Edition', 'Company Brand Guidelines v2', 'Chicago Manual of Style'.

notes.$comment: Flexible section for anything that doesn't fit elsewhere.

risks.$comment: Examples: 'Tight deadline', 'Pending client approval', 'Data collection delays'.

assumptions.description: Assumptions the project is based on.
assumptions.$comment: Examples: 'Client will provide brand assets by Nov 15', 'Survey will reach 100+ respondents'.

recommendations.description: Suggestions for improvement or next steps.
